tax: Placed at family rank, Gust Aeolians is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a spinning bounded-air body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a spinning air body with its own draught.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dust-laden spinning column. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — dust spiralling against the draught — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Gust Aeolians occupies vertical shafts and chimneys. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure spins a shaft draught and recur with the draught resumes, sustaining themselves on channelled draught.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: spins up to fling crews from ledges. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: spinning air body and air-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: flinging and lifting. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Gust Aeolians matters because flings crews from exposed ledges. It lets survey reports sort spinning-air hazards from settled-air ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Gust Aeolians is the family-rank grouping of fast spinning-air elementals of the whirlwind kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a spinning air-column body that flings crews and debris, and divide below into genus groups by shaft geometry and spin force. The fall hazard of every shaft.
